query: 现在openclaw的实现来看，是否从实现思路上 已经达到了 控制整个propmt？

transcript.content: 可是 每一轮的 Context(估算) 并没有减小啊
对，这就是现在最该警惕的误读点：`Context(估算)` 没明显变小，不等于 GC 没生效。 原因是现在有两个不同口径：
肉眼看到的 # Context History 也没有减小，一直增加，也是正常的？

是，通常正常。 `# Context History` 来自最近几轮的 `last_token_usage.input_tokens`，代码就在 [src/codex-vscode-context-minor-gc.js](/Users/redcreen/Project/unified-memory-core/src/codex-vscode-context-minor-gc.js:240) 和…
不对啊， 换了话题后，为什么宿主每伦的context 不能减小？
你这个质疑是对的。 如果从“产品体感”看，换了话题后，宿主每轮 context 当然应该更小；现在没变小，说明**宿主层并没有真正被裁掉**。不是你理解错了，是当前实现只做到了“项目层局部裁剪”，没做到“宿主层实际瘦身”。
需要，我写过最终目标一定是用户体感明显变化，使用时不需要出现compact，你现在这样做的方案不是在骗自己么？
对。按你定义的最终目标看，如果用户体感没明显变化，还需要 `compact` 才能控制上下文，那当前方案最多算“局部技术准备”，不能算成功。把它说成达标，就是在骗自己。 我建议把验收标准改成这 4 条，缺一条都算没过：
需要，并且现在需要评估下 宿主端 codex openclaw 两个，是否能够达到这个目标；
对。如果把“project 层 working-set 变薄”当成“最终产品目标已达成”，那就是在骗自己。你的最终目标写得很清楚：**用户体感要明显变化，正常使用时不需要 `compact`**。按这个标准，当前 Codex 这条路径还没过。 **可落文案** 可以直接放进文档：
现在openclaw的实现来看，是否从实现思路上 已经达到了 控制整个propmt？
结论：**从实现思路上，OpenClaw 已经达到了“控制 prompt 主干”的位置；但还没达到“控制宿主最终整个 prompt 的全部组成”。** 更准确地说，OpenClaw 现在控制的是这三块：

decision.archive_summary: Earlier resolved subtopic: the user challenged why estimated context and context-history did not visibly shrink after topic changes. Conclusion reached: current Codex-side GC only trims project-local working set and does not materially reduce host-side per-turn context, so visible shrinkage is not happening yet.
decision.reasoning_summary: The active topic is still the same evaluation thread: whether the implementation approach actually controls the overall prompt and meets the user's goal of obvious user-perceived context reduction without needing compact. The next turn will likely depend on the unresolved acceptance standard and the codex-vs-openclaw evaluation established in t7-t10, so those turns should remain raw. The earlier t1-t6 block is a resolved diagnostic subthread and is a good eviction candidate.

applied.archiveSummary: Earlier resolved subtopic: the user challenged why estimated context and context-history did not visibly shrink after topic changes. Conclusion reached: current Codex-side GC only trims project-local working set and does not materially reduce host-side per-turn context, so visible shrinkage is not happening yet.
applied.reasoningSummary: The active topic is still the same evaluation thread: whether the implementation approach actually controls the overall prompt and meets the user's goal of obvious user-perceived context reduction without needing compact. The next turn will likely depend on the unresolved acceptance standard and the codex-vs-openclaw evaluation established in t7-t10, so those turns should remain raw. The earlier t1-t6 block is a resolved diagnostic subthread and is a good eviction candidate.
